metadata.description: PostgreSQL (via Prisma, see apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma Shipment model) is the system of record for shipments. Firestore collections defined here are a read-optimized projection/cache used for real-time tracking in web/mobile clients. All canonical writes to shipments must go through the Express API, which is responsible for synchronizing changes from PostgreSQL into Firestore (e.g., via write-through logic, background workers, or functions). Firestore MUST NOT be treated as the authoritative source for shipment data.

collectionsRole.shipments: Cached projection of Shipment entities from PostgreSQL for low-latency reads and live updates.
collectionsRole.drivers: Real-time driver state associated to shipments; driver identifiers must correspond to drivers referenced from PostgreSQL where applicable.
collectionsRole.events: Time-ordered tracking and telemetry events linked to shipments; shipmentId must reference a Shipment that exists in PostgreSQL.
